title: Monopsonistic Markets
description: A monopsony exists when there is a single buyer of a a good, service, or factor of production. Practice what you have learned about how monopsony power affects how much of a factor is hired, the payment for that factor, and the impact on efficiency.
topic_path: Choosing inputs when factor markets are monopolistically competitive

assessment_items.question_content:
Hogwarts hires wizardry teachers in a monopsonistic labor market. The graph shown here illustrates Hogwarts’ marginal factor cost of labor $(MFC_L)$, the supply of labor $(S_L)$, and the marginal revenue product of labor $(MRP_L)$ for wizardry teachers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e092691e51a1bdec8e937f6439b0ef6dea757c0c)

**What combination of wage and quantity of labor will Hogwarts choose to maximize profits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A factory that makes hot glue guns hires labor in a monopsonistic competitive labor market. This graph illustrates the firm’s marginal factor cost of labor (MFC_L), marginal revenue product of labor $(MRP_L)$, and the supply of labor $(S_L)$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0a04703fb3d7a24c1fd55dc0f132c0c916509298)

**What wage and quantity combination will this firm choose to maximize profits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

This graph illustrates a firm’s marginal factor cost of labor $(MFC_L)$, marginal revenue product of labor $(MRP_L)$, and the supply of labor $(S_L)$. This firm hires labor in a monopsony labor market.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9eb404e5baecc2c2473b88aec722182cb7046239)

**What wage and quantity combination will this firm choose to maximize profits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Hogwarts hires wizardry teachers in a monopsonistic labor market. The graph shown here illustrates Hogwarts’ marginal factor cost of labor $(MFC_L)$, the supply of labor $(S_L)$, and the marginal revenue product of labor $(MRP_L)$ for wizardry teachers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0de1fb9df80793053966a2a4cf1c7f81c5ce28bd)

**What area represents deadweight loss in this monopsony labor market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

This graph illustrates a monopsony labor market.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/90b8678f97d8f74f61c72174b6e96a55ce8c71f4)

**What area represents deadweight loss in this market?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

This graph illustrates a firm’s marginal factor cost of labor $(MFC_L)$, marginal revenue product of labor $(MRP_L)$, and the supply of labor $(S_L)$. This firm hires labor in a monopsony labor market.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a8f265f25ab31218a6b58d0a85aea015b4386ee)

**What is deadweight loss in this market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Deadweight loss is represented by the area under the $MRP_L$ curve  and above the labor supply curve between the competitive hiring and monopsony hiring.
The competitive hiring is 600 workers and the monopsony hires 400 workers. The marginal factor cost is $\$55$, and the wage paid is $\$35$.

Deadweight lost can be found using this equation:

$DWL=\dfrac{1}{2} (\$55-\$25) \times (600-400)$

assessment_items.question_content:
Beth is an evil genius who hires workers to be her minions and carry out her dastardly deeds in a monopsonistically competitive labor market. She also wants to maximize profit. 

**When should Beth stop hiring workers, and how much should she pay them?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: stop hiring when the marginal factor cost of a worker equals the marginal revenue product of labor; pay a wage less than the marginal revenue product of labor
the supply of labor equals the marginal revenue product of labor, and pay a wage equal to the marginal revenue product of labor
the supply of labor equals the marginal revenue product of labor, and pay a wage greater than the marginal revenue product of labor
the marginal factor cost is less than the marginal revenue product of labor, and pay a wage less than the marginal revenue product of labor.
the marginal factor cost is less than the marginal revenue product of labor, and pay a wage rate greater than the marginal revenue product of labor.

assessment_items.question_content:
Hogwarts is the only firm hiring teachers. The labor supply of wizardry teachers is given in the market shown here.

Wage (per day) | Quantity of labor supplied 
-|-
$\$300$ | $0$
$\$400$ | $1$
$\$500$ | $2$
$\$600$ | $3$
$\$700$ | $4$

**What is the marginal factor cost of the second unit of labor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the marginal factor cost, you must first find the total cost of hiring $2$ units of labor.

The total cost of hiring $2$ units of labor is $\$1000$:

$\begin{align} \text{total cost of labor} &= \text{quantity of labor hired} \times \text{wage}\\\\
&=2 \times \$500\\\\
&=\$1000\end{align}$

To find the marginal cost of the second unit of labor, subtract the total cost of $1$ unit from the total cost of $2$ units. $1$ unit of labor costs $\$400$.

The marginal cost of the second unit of labor is $\$600$:

$\$1000 - \$400 = \$600$

assessment_items.question_content:
A single firm in Aluminum Valley hires computer coders. The market supply of computer coder labor is given here.

Wage (per hour) | Quantity of labor supplied | Total cost of labor
-|-|-
$\$10$ | $1$ | $\$10$
$\$20$ | $2$ | $\$40$
$\$30$ | $3$ | $\$90$
$\$40$ | $4$ | $\$160$
$\$50$ | $5$ | $\$250$


**What is the marginal factor cost of the fifth worker?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Blammo is the only firm in Pamtown where people are employed. The figure below represents the labor market in Pamtown.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fe61b18e5a9dff3df20b502b6e07e04f480b6390)

**If the government of Pamtown imposes a minimum wage of $\$45$ per day in this market, how much labor is employed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A minimum wage has been placed on a monopsony labor market that is greater than the monopsony wage, but less than the wage where the marginal revenue product of labor and the marginal factor cost of labor intersect. 

**What happens as a result of the minimum wage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What happens if a minimum wage is imposed in a monopsony labor market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The marginal factor cost becomes horizontal at the minimum wage until it hits the labor supply curve.
The labor supply curve becomes vertical at the amount of labor hired in a competitive market.
The demand for labor becomes vertical.
The demand for labor becomes horizontal.
The marginal factor cost curve is less than the labor supply curve.
